Motivation
Why I want to join
Revolut's Graduate Programme

I've spent the last three years doing one thing consistently: finding growth where others see a plateau. At Darty, I implemented a Click & Collect system that grew revenue by 75%. At Midea, I built workflows that made the after-sales team 43% faster. At Coach, I pushed average transaction value from €193 to €223 through intentional upselling.

Revolut is the place where those instincts get rewarded at scale. The Graduate Programme offers exactly what I need: a structured environment to sharpen my commercial edge, a product I genuinely believe in, and an international team that runs fast. I'm bilingual in French and English, fluent in Portuguese, and I'm not afraid of a cold call at 8am.

01.
I sell what I understand
Revolut Business solves real pain points I've seen firsthand — expense management, multi-currency operations, payment efficiency. That makes conversations authentic.
02.
I thrive in fast cycles
I'm used to building pipelines from zero, tracking KPIs daily, and iterating fast. The bootcamp-to-client model fits exactly how I learn — by doing.
03.
Data-first approach
Power BI, Salesforce, HubSpot — I work with tools that make every decision traceable. Solution-based selling starts with understanding the numbers, not ignoring them.

Things I've built
beyond the job description
End-to-end SAV digitalization
Redesigned the entire after-sales request process at Midea — paper forms replaced by automated HTML forms feeding Power Automate flows into shared Excel dashboards. Deployed for 1,000+ external distributors.
-43% processing time 1000+ users Live in production
Local market growth plan
Built a complete go-to-market strategy for the Istres store from scratch — customer segmentation, B2B account reactivation, social media presence, and the Click & Collect infrastructure that generated a 75% revenue jump.
+75% revenue +56% traffic B2B reactivation
KPI monitoring across regions
